name: I — Identity
description: Identity bird returns its argument unchanged: I x = x

name: K — Kestrel
description: Kestrel keeps the first, discards the second: K x y = x

name: KI — Kite
description: Kite discards the first, keeps the second: KI x y = y

name: M — Mockingbird
description: Mockingbird self-applies: M f = f f. M I = I I = I

name: B — Bluebird (Compose)
description: Bluebird composes functions: B f g x = f (g x). Here: (5*2)+1 = 11

name: C — Cardinal (Flip)
description: Cardinal flips arguments: C f x y = f y x

name: W — Warbler (Duplicate)
description: Warbler duplicates: W f x = f x x. Here: 5 + 5 = 10

name: S — Starling (Fork)
description: Starling forks: S f g x = f x (g x). Here: 3 + (3*2) = 9

name: V — Vireo (Pair)
description: Vireo pairs: V x y f = f x y. Use K to extract first, KI for second

name: L — Lark
description: Lark: L f g = f (g g). Combines composition with self-application

name: Y — Sage (Fixpoint)
description: Y combinator: Y f = f (Y f). Enables recursion without explicit self-reference

name: S K K = I
description: S K K reduces to identity. Proof: S K K x = K x (K x) = x

name: W K = I
description: W K x = K x x = x. Warbler-Kestrel is another Identity encoding

name: M I = I
description: M I = I I = I. Mockingbird-Identity stays identity

name: B f I = f
description: Right identity of composition: composing with identity is a no-op

name: B I f = f
description: Left identity of composition: identity composed with f is still f

name: C K = KI
description: C K x y = K y x = y. Cardinal-Kestrel = Kite

name: V pair selectors
description: V x y K = x, V x y KI = y — Vireo pairs with Kestrel/Kite select elements

name: Church Booleans
description: true = K (select first), false = KI (select second), not = C (flip)

name: Church Numerals
description: Numbers as repeated function application: n f x = f^n(x)

name: Church Pairs (Vireo)
description: Pairs built from V, linked list from nested pairs

name: Birds as data
description: Wrap bird combinators in attribute sets with metadata

name: Pipeline with B
description: Chain transformations using bluebird composition

name: Map with combinators
description: Use S and K to build map/filter from scratch

name: Recursive factorial (Y)
description: Y combinator enables anonymous recursion — no let rec needed

name: Import birds.nix
description: Import the canonical bird definitions directly

name: Bird speech
description: Each bird has a personality and explains what it does

name: Full library
description: Import the entire bird-nix library with all modules

name: Path graph
description: Generate a linear chain: n0 → n1 → n2 → n3 → n4

name: Star graph
description: Hub-and-spoke: center node connected to all spokes

name: Complete graph
description: Every node connected to every other node (K₄)

name: Cycle graph
description: Path with last node looping back to first

name: Grid graph
description: 2D lattice — each node connects right and down

name: Subdivide edge
description: Replace edge a→b with a→mid→b (insert intermediate node)

name: Hub rule
description: Add a central hub connected bidirectionally to all existing nodes

name: Reverse edges
description: Flip all directed edges (the Cardinal transformation)

name: Compose transforms
description: B (Bluebird) composes graph transformations sequentially

name: Social network
description: Friend graph with metadata — translated from social_network.ggl

name: Neural network layers
description: Input → Hidden → Output layer connectivity — from neural_network.ggl

name: HPC cluster topology
description: GPU/CPU compute nodes with scheduler — from hpc_network.ggl

name: Quantum circuit
description: Qubits and gates with entanglement edges — from quantum_circuit.ggl

name: Toroidal mesh
description: 6×6 torus network with wrap-around edges — from toroidal_mesh.ggl

name: Protein interaction network
description: p53/MDM2/ATM signaling pathway — from protein_network.ggl

name: Chemical reaction graph
description: H₂ + O₂ → H₂O catalyzed by Pt — from chemical_reaction.ggl
